cot_prefix: Okay, let's find the answer to this question. The question is asking what the father did when he was laid off. First, I'll read through the story. The story starts with the narrator coming home and finding his dad at home. He's surprised because his dad is laid off. The father says, "Andrew, I was laid off today," and the narrator thinks it's a joke at first. Then he realizes it's true. The story then mentions that the father was a hard worker who took pride in his career. After being laid off, his unemployment caused many changes. He was home all day, the narrator had to clean up, do homework, etc. The father spent time on the computer looking for jobs. The narrator noticed how down and affected his self-esteem was, even though he tried to be optimistic. The father asked the kids to spend less money, the narrator gave up his allowance and got a part-time job. After months of searching, the father decided to start his own business. The narrator helped him and learned a lot from him. The question is about what the father did when he was laid off. The options are: (A) was angry with his boss (B) didn't care about it at all (C) couldn't accept the fact easily (D) was as happy as usual From the story, the father initially was quiet when he told his son. The narrator thought he was joking at first, then saw his expression and realized he was serious. The father's unemployment affected his self-esteem, and he became down. This suggests he didn't take the layoff easily. He tried to look for jobs, started a business later, and tried to be optimistic. So, he was affected and couldn't easily accept the layoff. That matches with option C, which says he couldn't accept the fact easily. Options A and D don't match the story. The story doesn't mention anger towards the boss, nor does it say he was happy.
